positiveTerminal: Work clamp connects to negative (-) terminal. Weld gun/MIG cable connects to positive (+) terminal (Euro connector).
notes: DCEP = Direct Current Electrode Positive. The MIG gun is the electrode and connects to the positive terminal.

positiveTerminal: Work clamp connects to positive (+) terminal. Weld gun/MIG cable connects to negative (-) terminal.
notes: DCEN = Direct Current Electrode Negative. Polarity is REVERSED from MIG. Must switch leads at the front panel terminals.

positiveTerminal: Same as MIG setup. Work clamp to negative, gun to positive.
notes: Gas-shielded flux-cored uses the same polarity as MIG (DCEP).

positiveTerminal: Work clamp connects to positive (+) terminal.
negativeTerminal: TIG torch connects to negative (-) terminal using the TIG adapter/dinse connector.
notes: DCEN = Direct Current Electrode Negative. DC TIG only (no AC TIG on this machine). Use the TIG torch dinse connector in the negative terminal.

positiveTerminal: Depends on electrode type. Most electrodes (E6013, E7018) use DCEP: electrode holder to positive, work clamp to negative.
negativeTerminal: Some electrodes (E6011) can use DCEN: electrode holder to negative, work clamp to positive.
notes: Check electrode packaging for recommended polarity. E6013 = DCEP or AC. E7018 = DCEP. E6010/E6011 = DCEP.
